medications: Sumatriptan 50 mg as needed for migraines
Naproxen 220 mg as needed for migraine pain
Ferrous sulfate 325 mg every other day
Cetirizine 10 mg daily during allergy season
health.mental_health: Actively grieving the recent death of her older brother; experiencing waves of sadness, irritability, and guilt, with sleep disruption and occasional panic-like shortness of breath that overlaps with asthma. Considering short-term grief counseling but hasn’t told most friends.
recent_visits: Primary care follow-up for fatigue and low ferritin; advised iron regimen and repeat labs in 8 weeks
Urgent care visit after a severe migraine with nausea and light sensitivity
Pulmonology telehealth check-in to adjust asthma controller after winter flare-ups
First intake call with a community grief support group coordinator (not yet attended)

finance.income_range: $38,000–$52,000/year (entry-level UX designer in Detroit; some freelance on the side)
finance.debt: About $14,000 in federal student loans from switching out of a university program into a shorter design track; also carrying ~$1,200 on a credit card after contributing to funeral travel costs and covering extra household bills for her mom.
investments: High-yield savings account (emergency fund in progress)
recent_transactions: $620 round-trip flight to Chicago for memorial and family logistics
$180 co-pay and lab fees for anemia follow-up
$240 sent via Zelle to her mom for utilities
$36.78 pharmacy purchase (iron supplements and inhaler refill)

relationships: Ewa Kowalski (mom) — close but both emotionally raw; Chioma is helping with paperwork and bills
Marek Kowalski (stepdad) — cordial relationship; tension around how to talk about the loss
Adaeze Okafor (best friend since high school) — main confidant; checks in daily
Kofi Mensah (coworker friend) — supportive at work, but Chioma keeps details limited
Extended family in Chicago — increased contact due to the death; some conflict about funeral decisions
social.social_media_activity: Mostly lurks on Instagram and TikTok; posted one brief story about taking time to grieve without naming the family member. Mutes group chats when migraines hit and avoids commenting on family threads to prevent arguments.
private_conversations: Discussing whether to start grief counseling and fear of being seen as 'not coping'
Family disagreement about funeral expenses and who should pay what
Telling her best friend about guilt for missing a final phone call
Explaining to her manager that migraines and grief are affecting concentration

schedule.work_hours: Mon–Fri, 9:30am–5:30pm (hybrid: in-office Tue/Thu in Downtown Detroit; remote other days)
regular_appointments: Monthly PCP lab check scheduled until iron levels stabilize
Asthma controller refill check-in every 3 months
Weekly evening grief support group (tentative, starting next week)
Biweekly freelance client call (Saturday late morning)
upcoming_events: HR meeting to finalize bereavement leave paperwork
Dentist cleaning (rescheduled twice due to migraines)
Family visit to her mom’s house to sort photos and documents
Product design critique session with her team (in-person Thursday)
schedule.daily_routine: Wakes around 7:45am, tries to eat something iron-friendly before work, keeps lights low when migraines start, uses an inhaler before long walks in cold air, and often ends evenings organizing family paperwork or scrolling quietly when sleep won’t come.

professional.company: Mid-size healthcare software company in Detroit building patient scheduling and billing tools
projects: Redesigning the patient appointment booking flow to reduce drop-off
Accessibility audit and color-contrast updates for a clinician dashboard
Usability testing plan for a new bill-pay experience (working with a researcher and PM)
professional.performance_issues: Since the death in her family, she’s had a few late mornings, difficulty focusing during critiques, and missed one internal deadline after a two-day migraine. She requested flexible scheduling and reduced meeting load for a month but worries it will affect her reputation as a new hire.
workplace_relationships: Manager (Sonia Patel) — supportive but expects clear updates; Chioma shares limited personal details
Product manager (Ethan Brooks) — pushes timelines; some tension when Chioma asks for extensions
Design teammate (Marisol Vega) — helps cover meetings on migraine days; friendly and discreet

hobbies: Sketching UI ideas in a notebook and making mini design systems
Photography walks along the RiverWalk when weather allows
Thrifting in Midtown and upcycling clothes
Cooking simple Nigerian-inspired meals with her mom on weekends
habits: Tracks migraine triggers (sleep, dehydration, screen brightness) in a notes app
Keeps an inhaler in her tote and one at her desk
Late-night scrolling when grief spikes, which worsens insomnia
Avoids alcohol most of the time because it can trigger migraines
Sometimes skips meals when stressed, contributing to fatigue
lifestyle.living_situation: Lives with her mom in a small rental on Detroit’s west side; staying there more consistently since the death to help with errands, paperwork, and emotional support, even though it reduces her independence.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Tries to eat iron-rich foods (spinach, beans, red meat occasionally) and pairs iron supplements with vitamin C; limits caffeine and very salty takeout because it can worsen headaches and dehydration.
